plan_metadata.name: DA Api Key js Local Debug

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu to select it.
Click on the dropdown suggestion "Create a declarative agent with an action" within the "Add an action to your declarative agent" input field in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog.
Click the "Start with a New API" dropdown option in the "Create an Action" menu of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "API" option in the "Authentication Type" dropdown menu located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the dropdown menu under the "Programming Language" field in Visual Studio Code to select it.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the project root location.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
cd /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_4HybD
echo "Checking for package.json..."
if [ ! -f package.json ]; then
  echo "Error: package.json not found. Aborting."
  exit 1
fi
npm run keygen > keygen_output.txt
API_KEY=$(grep -oP 'Generated a new API Key: \K.*' keygen_output.txt | head -1)
echo "Extracted API Key: $API_KEY"
if [ -z "$API_KEY" ]; then
  echo "API Key not found in npm output. Aborting."
  exit 2
fi
ENV_FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_4HybD/env/.env.local.user"
echo "Verifying env file path..."
if [ ! -f "$ENV_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: ENV file $ENV_FILE not found. Aborting."
  exit 3
fi
sed -i "s|SECRET_API_KEY=[ ]*['\"]*.*['\"]*|SECRET_API_KEY='$API_KEY'|" "$ENV_FILE"
echo "Updated ENV file with new API key."
cat "$ENV_FILE"
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Checking for package.json...
Extracted API Key: rBAuaG5VrBaH
Verifying env file path...
Updated ENV file with new API key.
# This file includes environment variables that will not be committed to git by default. You can set these environment variables in your CI/CD system for your project.

# Secrets. Keys prefixed with `SECRET_` will be masked in Teams Toolkit logs.
SECRET_API_KEY='rBAuaG5VrBaH'

✓ Script executed successfully

=== STDERR ===

Success

steps.description: @code run "npm run keygen" at /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}. fill in the "Generated a new API Key: <value>" into /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.local.user, replace SECRET_API_KEY= ' ' with the  <value> and save the file.
Click on the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the file named ".env.local.user" located under the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Close" (Ctrl+W) button on the `.env.local.user` tab in the Visual Studio Code editor interface.
